header.description: Message an assistant or GPT, analyze images, generate audio, etc.

generate.description: Creates a video from a text prompt

translate.description: Translates audio into text in English

deleteFile.description: Delete a file from the server

list.description: Returns a list of files that belong to the user's organization

upload.description: Upload a file that can be used across various endpoints

analyze.description: Take in images and answer questions about them

response.description: Generate a model response with GPT 3, 4, 5, etc. using Responses API

classify.description: Check whether content complies with usage policies

fileSizeLimitNotice.displayName: OpenAI API limits the size of the audio file to 25 MB

model.description: The model to use for image generation

input.description: The input text to classify if it is violates the moderation policy

voice.description: The voice to use when generating the audio

language.description: The language of the input audio. Supplying the input language in ISO-639-1 format will improve accuracy and latency.

temperature.description: What sampling temperature to use, between 0 and 2. Higher values like 0.8 will make the output more random, while lower values like 0.2 will make it more focused and deterministic. We generally recommend altering this or top_p but not both

purpose.description: Only return files with the given purpose

dalleQuality.description: The quality of the image that will be generated, HD creates images with finer details and greater consistency across the image

quality.description: The quality of the image that will be generated, High creates images with finer details and greater consistency across the image

vivid.description: Lean towards generating hyper-real and dramatic images

returnImageUrls.description: Whether to return image URL(s) instead of binary file(s)

auto.description: Model will look at the image input size and decide if it should use the low or high setting

low.description: Return faster responses and consume fewer tokens

high.description: Return more detailed responses, consumes more tokens

maxTokens.description: The maximum number of tokens to generate in the completion. Most models have a context length of 2048 tokens (except for the newest models, which support 32,768).

user.description: A unique identifier representing your end-user, which can help OpenAI to monitor and detect abuse

background.description: Allows to set transparency for the background of the generated image(s). Only supported for gpt-image-1.

inputFidelity.description: Control how much effort the model will exert to match the style and features of input images. Only supported for gpt-image-1.

imageMask.description: Name of the binary property which contains the image. An additional image whose fully transparent areas (e.g. where alpha is zero) indicate where image should be edited. If there are multiple images provided, the mask will be applied on the first image. Must be a valid PNG file, less than 4MB, and have the same dimensions as image.

useStableModel.description: Whether to use the stable version of the model instead of the latest version, accuracy may be slightly lower

conversationId.description: The conversation that this response belongs to. Input items and output items from this response are automatically added to this conversation after this response completes.

include.description: Specify additional output data to include in the model response

instructions.description: Instructions for the model to follow

maxToolsIterations.description: The maximum number of tool iteration cycles the LLM will run before stopping. A single iteration can contain multiple tool calls. Set to 0 for no limit.

maxToolCalls.description: The maximum number of total calls to built-in tools that can be processed in a response. This maximum number applies across all built-in tool calls, not per individual tool. Any further attempts to call a tool by the model will be ignored.

metadata.description: Set of 16 key-value pairs that can be attached to an object. This can be useful for storing additional information about the object in a structured format, and querying for objects via API or the dashboard. Keys are strings with a maximum length of 64 characters. Values are strings with a maximum length of 512 characters.

parallelToolCalls.description: Whether to allow parallel tool calls. If true, the model can call multiple tools at once.

previousResponseId.description: The ID of the previous response to continue from. Cannot be used in conjunction with Conversation ID.

promptConfig.description: Configure the reusable prompt template configured via OpenAI Dashboard. Learn more.

promptId.description: The unique identifier of the prompt template to use

version.description: Optional version of the prompt template

variables.description: Variables to be substituted into the prompt template

promptId.description: The unique identifier of the prompt template to use

version.description: Optional version of the prompt template

variables.description: Variables to be substituted into the prompt template

promptCacheKey.description: Used by OpenAI to cache responses for similar requests to optimize your cache hit rates

summary.description: A summary of the reasoning performed by the model. This can be useful for debugging and understanding the model's reasoning process.
A summary of the reasoning performed by the model. This can be useful for debugging and understanding the model's reasoning process.

safetyIdentifier.description: A stable identifier used to help detect users of your application that may be violating OpenAI's usage policies. The IDs should be a string that uniquely identifies each user.

serviceTier.description: The service tier to use for the request

store.description: Whether to store the generated model response for later retrieval via API

name.description: The name of the response format. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.

requiredNotice.displayName: All properties in the schema must be set to "required", when using "strict" mode.

schema.description: The schema of the response format

description.description: The description of the response format

strict.description: Whether to require that the AI will always generate responses that match the provided JSON Schema

name.description: The name of the response format. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.

requiredNotice.displayName: All properties in the schema must be set to "required", when using "strict" mode.

schema.description: The schema of the response format

description.description: The description of the response format

strict.description: Whether to require that the AI will always generate responses that match the provided JSON Schema

topLogprobs.description: An integer between 0 and 20 specifying the number of most likely tokens to return at each token position, each with an associated log probability

topP.description: An alternative to sampling with temperature, controls diversity via nucleus sampling: 0.5 means half of all likelihood-weighted options are considered. We generally recommend altering this or temperature but not both.

truncation.description: Whether to truncate the input to the model's context window size. When disabled will throw a 400 error instead.

enabled.description: Whether to run the model in background mode. If true, the model will run in background mode.

timeout.description: The timeout for the background mode in seconds. If 0, the timeout is infinite.

enabled.description: Whether to run the model in background mode. If true, the model will run in background mode.

timeout.description: The timeout for the background mode in seconds. If 0, the timeout is infinite.

binaryPropertyNameReference.description: Optional image reference that guides generation

waitTime.description: Time to wait for the video to be generated in seconds

binaryPropertyName.description: Name of the binary property which contains the image. It should be a square png file less than 4MB.

prompt.description: The prompt to generate a video from

imageUrls.description: URL(s) of the image(s) to analyze, multiple URLs can be added separated by comma

simplify.description: Whether to return a simplified version of the response instead of the raw data

images.description: Add one or more binary fields to include images with your prompt. Each image should be a png, webp, or jpg file less than 50MB. You can provide up to 16 images.

binaryPropertyName.description: The name of the binary field containing the image data
The name of the binary field containing the image data

n.description: The number of images to generate. Must be between 1 and 10.

size.description: Output resolution formatted as width x height. 1024x1792 and 1792x1024 are only supported by Sora 2 Pro.

quality.description: The quality of the image that will be generated

responseFormat.description: The format in which the generated images are returned. URLs are only valid for 60 minutes after generation.

outputFormat.description: The format in which the generated images are returned. Only supported for gpt-image-1.

outputCompression.description: The compression level (0-100%) for the generated images. Only supported for gpt-image-1 with webp or jpeg output formats.

role.description: Role in shaping the model's response, it tells the model how it should behave and interact with the user

user.description: Send a message as a user and get a response from the model

assistant.description: Tell the model to adopt a specific tone or personality

system.description: Usually used to set the model's behavior or context for the next user message

content.description: The content of the message to be send
content.placeholder: e.g. Hello, how can you help me?

imageUrl.description: URL of the image to be sent

binaryPropertyName.description: Name of the binary property which contains the file

fileId.description: ID of the file to be sent

imageDetail.description: The detail level of the image to be sent to the model

fileUrl.description: URL of the file to be sent. Accepts base64 encoded files as well.

assistant.description: Tell the model to adopt a specific tone or personality

fileUrl.description: URL of the file to be sent. Accepts base64 encoded files as well.

noticeTools.displayName: Connect your own custom n8n tools to this node on the canvas

searchContextSize.description: High level guidance for the amount of context window space to use for the search

allowedDomains.description: Comma-separated list of domains to search. Only domains in this list will be searched.

vectorStoreIds.description: The vector store IDs to use for the file search. Vector stores are managed via OpenAI Dashboard.

codeInterpreter.description: Whether to allow the model to execute code in a sandboxed environment

role.description: Role in shaping the model's response, it tells the model how it should behave and interact with the user

user.description: Send a message as a user and get a response from the model

assistant.description: Tell the model to adopt a specific tone or personality

system.description: Usually used to set the model's behavior or context for the next user message

content.description: The content of the message to be send
content.placeholder: e.g. Hello, how can you help me?

conversationId.description: The ID of the conversation to retrieve
